title: El Mundial quedó a deber en México
summary: Las ventas de tiendas ANTAD cayeron 1.6% en junio frente al mismo mes de 2025 —la primera contracción anual desde febrero de 2025—, una caída que se amplía a 4.8% en términos reales al descontar la inflación. ANTAD atribuyó el resultado a un efecto de calendario y al Mundial 2026, que modificó temporalmente los hábitos de consumo y redujo la afluencia a tiendas físicas.

title: Asia AI bets power record equities run for Wall Street banks
summary: Investments tied to Asian semiconductor and AI bets are fueling record regional equities revenue for US banks. The trend shows AI enthusiasm continuing to flow through to Wall Street's trading and investment-banking results despite growing volatility in AI-linked stocks.

title: The next crash: why this time might not be different
summary: The FT argues that stock markets are ignoring obvious risks and remain imbued with extreme optimism, raising the question of whether investors are set up for another painful surprise. The piece warns current conditions echo prior periods of complacency before major downturns.

title: Las claves económicas de la semana (13 al 17 de julio): De líneas de pobreza e inflación en EU
summary: Resumen semanal de indicadores clave para México y el mundo: los mexicanos necesitaron 4,888 pesos en junio para acceder a la canasta básica, mientras en EU se publicaron datos de inflación al consumidor y productor y la Fed difundió su Libro Beige. Los Cetes a 28 días tocaron además su nivel más bajo desde mediados de 2022.

title: China Dismisses Claim that It Illicitly Extracts Foreign AI Tech
summary: China rejected allegations from US AI firms, including Anthropic, that Chinese rivals are illicitly distilling results from top American models to advance their own capabilities. The dispute adds to rising US-China friction over AI development and intellectual property.

title: Baker Tilly Eyes $3 Billion in Debt to Replace Private Credit
summary: Deutsche Bank is preparing to sell about $3 billion in debt for accounting firm Baker Tilly Advisory Group, aimed at refinancing existing private credit loans. The deal illustrates a growing trend of companies tapping public debt markets to replace pricier private credit.

title: Gauge of Oracle’s Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
summary: A gauge of Oracle's credit risk hit a fresh all-time high as investors grew concerned about the company's aggressive cash burn and the future profitability of its AI investments. Worries were compounded by a new Chinese AI model that could rival offerings from OpenAI.

title: AI Upheaval Crushes Retail Traders Crowding Into Leveraged Funds
summary: A surprise AI breakthrough from Chinese startup Moonshot rippled through global markets, sending AI and semiconductor stocks sharply lower and hitting retail traders crowded into leveraged funds. The episode underscored how fast markets are being reshaped and how quickly assumptions about tomorrow's winners can change.

title: What Is Moonshot AI? Why China’s New Model Is Roiling Markets
summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI released a powerful new model on July 17 that drew global attention for its capabilities and triggered a sharp selloff in stock markets worldwide. The episode revived comparisons to last year's 'DeepSeek moment,' raising fresh doubts about returns on massive AI infrastructure spending.

title: Meta in Talks to Sell Computing Power to Anthropic
summary: Meta is in early talks to lease computing power from its data centers to Anthropic, an opportunity to build a new business around its heavy investment in AI infrastructure. The talks would deepen ties between two major AI players as demand for compute continues to outstrip supply.

title: Moonshot sacude a Wall Street: qué es Kimi K3 y por qué revive el temor a un “momento DeepSeek”
summary: El lanzamiento de Kimi K3, el nuevo modelo de IA del startup chino Moonshot, presionó a las tecnológicas y semiconductoras al reabrir el debate sobre si las inversiones multimillonarias en inteligencia artificial generarán la rentabilidad que el mercado espera. Analistas lo interpretan como un nuevo 'momento DeepSeek', que demuestra que China sigue reduciendo la brecha tecnológica con EU a menor costo.

title: AI-Based Businesses Are Diversifying and Rejecting AI Model Monogamy
summary: Businesses that depend on a single AI model are increasingly viewed as vulnerable, prompting many enterprises to diversify across multiple providers. The shift reflects growing caution about vendor lock-in as the competitive AI landscape shifts quickly.

title: Ralentiza la 4T el crecimiento
summary: Pese a un posible repunte del PIB en el cuarto trimestre, México cerrará 2026 con una economía que ha crecido a una tasa media anual de apenas 0.8% en los últimos ocho años. El dato refleja el estancamiento estructural del crecimiento mexicano pese a episodios de recuperación puntual.

title: Slim compra participación en campo petrolero de México a TotalEnergies
summary: Carlos Slim, a través de Grupo Carso, comprará el 30% de participación de TotalEnergies en el Bloque 30 del Golfo de México, que contiene el yacimiento KAN operado por Harbour Energy. La transacción, sujeta a autorizaciones gubernamentales, amplía la presencia de Slim en el sector petrolero mexicano.

title: Xi Seeks Global AI Sway as Surging Chinese Models Spur Alarm
summary: President Xi Jinping is using the rapid rise of China's AI models to stake a claim on shaping the technology's global rules. The growing power of Chinese AI models is stirring security concerns in both Washington and Beijing.

title: Chip Stocks Slide Into Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers accelerated, pushing the sector into a bear market on worries that the AI spending spree is becoming harder to justify. The move unwound a chunk of the blistering AI-led rally of the past few months.

title: CoreLogic Wraps Up $5.25 Billion Debt Deal With Hefty Yields
summary: CoreLogic raised $5.25 billion in a debt sale after amending terms and offering some of the year's highest yields, reflecting lukewarm investor demand. The deal underscores the elevated cost of credit facing real estate and mortgage-data players even as they refinance existing obligations.

title: Refurbishment Popocatépetl 35 / Studio Font
summary: A renovation of a 1920s-era building on Avenida Ámsterdam in Mexico City's Hipódromo Condesa neighborhood reinterprets the district's distinctive curved, racetrack-derived urban layout. The project by Studio Font references the area's origins as the capital's former horse racetrack.

title: How to shrink the Fed’s $7trn balance-sheet
summary: The Economist examines the challenge facing new Fed chair Kevin Warsh in trying to shrink the central bank's $7 trillion balance sheet. Reversing the effects of years of bond-buying will prove difficult even as policymakers seek to normalize monetary policy.
